prompt:
The nav logo "Yilan Gao" is wrapping to two lines when the viewport gets
squeezed. This should never be collapsed or moved to the next line. It
should never compromise; this is a principle. Anything that's logo or
navigation, such a core identity line, should never ever be returned to
two lines when it's squeezed. The personal identity blurb, which is the
'Designer, AI systems experience...', that line CAN be squeezed into two
lines — that's fine. Fix the nav and codify the priority rule.
response:
## Diagnosis The nav logo "Yilan Gao" is wrapping because it lacks `whitespace-nowrap` protection and the flex container is allowing it to shrink. When viewport pressure mounts, flex children negotiate space and the logo loses — it has no explicit instruction that it is non-negotiable. The identity blurb is correctly allowed to wrap. **Root cause:** Missing `whitespace-nowrap` + `flex-shrink-0` on the logo node, combined with no codified layout priority rule in the design system. This is **AP-072** (Unprotected Identity Elements in Flex Containers) — core brand marks treated as ordinary flex children with no shrink protection. --- ## Fix ### 1. Nav component — protect the logo, allow blurb to wrap ```tsx // components/Nav.tsx (or wherever your nav lives) // BEFORE — logo has no shrink protection
Yilan Gao Designer, AI systems experience...
{/* nav links */}
// AFTER — logo is non-negotiable, blurb may wrap
{/* Level-0: Core identity mark — whitespace-nowrap is non-negotiable */} Yilan Gao {/* Level-1: Identity blurb — permitted to wrap under pressure */} Designer, AI systems experience...
{/* nav links — these negotiate, logo does not */}
{/* ... */}
``` --- ### 2. Codify the rule in the design system Create the canonical source of truth for this principle so every future contributor sees it before touching layout. ```md # Layout Priority Levels These levels govern how elements behave under viewport pressure. Elements at a higher level **never** yield space to lower-level siblings. | Level | Name | Behaviour under pressure | Examples | |-------|------------------|-----------------------------------|-----------------------------------| | 0 | Identity | **Never wraps. Never shrinks.** | Logo text, brand name, monogram | | 1 | Identity blurb | May wrap. Never truncates. | Tagline, role descriptor | | 2 | Primary nav | May collapse to icon/hamburger. | Nav links, CTAs | | 3 | Secondary nav | Hides first. | Utility links, language switcher | | 4 | Content | Fully fluid. | Body copy, cards, images | ## Enforcement rules ### Level-0 (Identity) — non-negotiable contract Every element marked level-0 **must** carry: ```tsx // Tailwind className="whitespace-nowrap flex-shrink-0" // Or the Elan utility class (see below) className="identity-lock" ``` These two classes are **never** to be removed without an explicit design decision logged in DECISIONS.md. A linter rule enforces this (see below). ### Level-1 (Identity blurb) — soft wrap permitted May wrap naturally. Must never be truncated with `overflow-hidden` / `text-ellipsis`. The message must always be fully readable, just possibly taller. --- ## The Principle (verbatim, for code review and onboarding) > "Core identity lines — logo, brand name, personal name mark — must > never return to two lines under any viewport condition. They are > non-negotiable. Anything else can adapt; these cannot." > > — Design principle, yilangao.com ``` --- ### 3. Elan utility class — make the rule one word ```css /* design-system/elan.css (or your global CSS entry point) */ /* * .identity-lock * Level-0 layout priority — see design-system/tokens/layout-priority.md * Apply to: logo text, brand name, any core identity mark. * Contract: this element NEVER wraps, NEVER shrinks. * Do not remove without a logged design decision. */ @utility identity-lock { white-space: nowrap; flex-shrink: 0; min-width: max-content; } ``` Then your nav becomes self-documenting: ```tsx Yilan Gao ``` --- ### 4. Lightweight lint guard ```js // .eslintrc.js (or eslint.config.mjs) // Prevent anyone from adding `whitespace-normal` or `flex-shrink` // to an element that also carries `identity-lock`, catching accidental // overrides at commit time. { rules: { "no-restricted-syntax": [ "error", { // Catch: className strings that pair identity-lock with wrap overrides selector: "JSXAttribute[name.name='className'] Literal[value=/identity-lock/][value=/whitespace-normal|flex-shrink(?!-0)/]", message: "identity-lock elements must not carry wrap or shrink overrides. " + "See design-system/tokens/layout-priority.md → Level-0.", }, ], }, } ``` --- ## Principle (codified) > **Layout Priority Level-0 — Identity Lock** > Core identity marks (logo, name, brand text) carry `identity-lock` (`whitespace-nowrap` + `flex-shrink-0` + `min-width: max-content`). They are **layout constants, not layout participants**. Every other element negotiates around them. This is enforced by utility class, documentation, and lint — not by convention alone.
